summary:
The patient has chronic apical periodontitis. Treatment involves placing mineral trioxide aggregate (MTA) to seal and regenerate the area effectively after failed root canal therapy.
The definitive treatment aims at sealing off the infection with MTA and promoting healing through bone regrowth around the affected tooth’s tip. To summarize:

- Chronic apical periodontitis exists post-failed RCT.
- Mineral Tri oxide Aggregate (MTA) will be used for effective sealing and potential bone regeneration. Sentence count: 2
Last modified: June 30, 2023 At 8:57 PM Summary revised based on feedback provided earlier today by Dr. Smith during rounds.
